mission.title: Install and Configure K3s (Lightweight Kubernetes)
mission.description: K3s is a lightweight Kubernetes distribution designed for resource-constrained environments, making it ideal for edge computing, IoT, and development scenarios. It simplifies the installation process by packaging Kubernetes as a single binary, but may lack some advanced features found in full Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install K3s
steps.description:
This command installs K3s as a single binary on your server, setting up a lightweight Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -s - --k3s-version v1.31.0+k3s1

```

> Installs K3s version 1.31.0 with the default options.

steps.title: Verify K3s Installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the K3s service to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

systemctl status k3s

```

> This command shows the status of the K3s service.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Copy the Kubeconfig file to your home directory for easy access to the cluster.

```bash

sudo cp /etc/rancher/k3s/k3s.yaml ~/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config

```

> This allows you to use kubectl commands without specifying the config file.
> Ensure the Kubeconfig file is owned by your user.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your K3s cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes in the cluster and their statuses.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up additional features like metrics server and RBAC for enhanced functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/metrics-server/releases/latest/download/components.yaml

```

> Installs the metrics server for resource monitoring.

uninstall.title: Uninstall K3s
uninstall.description:
This command removes K3s and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all workloads and data stored in the cluster.

```bash

sudo /usr/local/bin/k3s-uninstall.sh

```

> This script removes K3s and cleans up the system.

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
After uninstalling, check for any orphaned resources that may need manual deletion.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces

```

> List all resources to identify any remaining items.

upgrade.title: Upgrade K3s
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your current configuration and workloads. After the upgrade, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > k3s-backup.yaml

curl -sfL https://get.k3s.io | sh -s - --k3s-version v1.31.0+k3s1

kubectl get nodes

```

> Backup current configurations.
> Upgrade K3s to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in a Ready state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'k3s is not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** K3s service may not have started correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the service status with 'systemctl status k3s' and review logs with 'journalctl -u k3s'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or permissions may be incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Kubeconfig file is copied correctly and owned by the user with 'sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) ~/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The container image may not be available or the node may lack internet access.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure the node has internet access.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The node may not have sufficient resources or may be misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check node resource allocation and logs for kubelet with 'journalctl -u k3s-agent'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new CNI plugins, improved metrics server integration.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for certain legacy storage backends.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to migrate any legacy storage configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with their statuses as 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
